description: Palatalization (specifically secondary palatalization or 'softening') is a phonetic process where the middle of the tongue is raised toward the hard palate during the articulation of a consonant. This is a hallmark of Slavic languages like Russian and often occurs before front vowels or palatal glides.
sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.

languages.description: Palatalization is a key feature of Russian phonology, distinguishing between 'hard' /d/ and 'soft' (palatalized) /dʲ/.
